records.ety: [F. rputer, L. reputare to count over, think over; pref. re- re- + putare to count, think. See Putative.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Reputed; p. pr. & vb. n. Reputing.]

defs: To hold in thought; to account; to estimate; to hold; to think; to reckon.
quotes: Wherefore are we counted as beasts, and reputed vile in your sight?
The king your father was reputed for A prince most prudent.

defs: Character reputed or attributed; reputation, whether good or bad; established opinion; public estimate.
quotes: He who regns Monarch in heaven, till then as one secure Sat on his throne, upheld by old repute.

defs: Specifically: Good character or reputation; credit or honor derived from common or public opinion; -- opposed to disrepute.
